case_name: Keyla Connie v. Robert P. Garnett

summary.brief: Keyla Connie v. Robert P. Garnett. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Connie, a 29-year-old woman, presented to the DHA emergency room on the night of October 11, 2014, with complaints of acute lower right leg pain.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: She was evaluated by Turner, a physician assistant, and the physical examination showed bilateral pulses/normal pulses.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Turner ordered a venous duplex ultrasound based on suspicion of possible deep vein thrombosis (“DVT”).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Three days later, Connie presented to Augusta University Medical Center with swelling and discoloration in her right foot and worsening foot pain.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: She was diagnosed with right lower extremity ischemia and underwent an open tibial thrombectomy that day.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Connie’s foot was ultimately partially amputated in December 2014.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court granted summary judgment in favor of Garnett, and Connie now appeals.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court found that Connie “failed to produce sufficient evidence showing Dr. Garnett violated the standard of care or that the alleged negligence was the cause of [Connie’s] damages.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Causation is established through expert testimony because the question of whether the alleged professional negligence caused the plaintiff’s injury is generally one for specialized expert knowledge.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Summary judgment is proper when there is no genuine issue of material fact and the movant is entitled to judgment as a matter of law.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Judgment affirmed in part and reversed in part.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports pain as nine out of ten.

reveal.clinical_significance: Does not rule out arterial occlusion

requestables.name: Arterial Duplex Scan
requestables.description: Scan to assess arterial blood flow

reveal.decision_impact: Could have led to timely intervention

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to DHA with acute symptoms

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with acute lower right leg pain
decision_points.prompt: What imaging investigation should be ordered?

options.clinical_reasoning: Necessary to rule out arterial occlusion

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant ordered venous duplex ultrasound only

expected_action_court.description: Arterial duplex scan should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established need for arterial assessment

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to assess arterial flow led to delayed diagnosis
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Arterial duplex scan would have identified occlusion

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed right lower extremity ischemia

key_findings: Insufficient evidence of breach of standard of care by Dr. Garnett
Causation not established against Dr. Garnett

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Arterial scan would have shown occlusion

factual_timeline.event: Patient presents to DHA with acute lower right leg pain
Patient presents to Augusta University Medical Center with worsening symptoms

allegations.allegation: Failure to order appropriate arterial imaging

court_findings.finding: No breach of standard of care by Dr. Garnett
court_findings.legal_significance: Summary judgment in favor of Garnett
